roadmap.vision: Build the Gyro ecosystem from theory to runtime, application, project operations, and research platform.

phases.name: Theory
phases.next: Complete and release Gyro Logic v3.1

phases.name: Application
phases.status: Primary and Applied Publication Milestones Completed / Future Evaluation
phases.next: Maintain publication metadata and move GyroAuth into post-publication evaluation and integration

phases.name: Ecosystem
phases.next: Reflect project updates and maintain weekly continuity

phases.name: Automation
phases.next: Evaluate DOI and publication synchronization tooling

future_research_themes.name: Mathematical Foundations
future_research_themes.objective: Establish rigorous mathematical objects and relations for Gyro Logic concepts without changing the invariant Core.
topics: Relations among Structure, Slice, Stability, Difference, and Trajectory
Potential relationships with topology and topological data analysis
future_research_themes.boundary: Candidate mathematical frameworks are research directions, not adopted foundations. Any formalization must preserve Structure → Slice → Stability unless Gyro Logic itself explicitly revises its theory.

future_research_themes.name: Gyro Runtime Architecture
future_research_themes.objective: Clarify GyroOS as a runtime for executing and evaluating state-transition processes rather than as a replacement for a conventional operating-system kernel.
topics: Relationship with Linux, microkernels, workflow runtimes, and AI runtimes
Measurable safety, continuity, and operational advantages
Naming and positioning of GyroOS versus Gyro Runtime
future_research_themes.boundary: This theme does not rename GyroOS or change its current architecture. It records the need to demonstrate the necessary interface between Gyro Logic and runtime execution.

future_research_themes.name: Trajectory-Based Authentication
future_research_themes.objective: Strengthen the formal and practical distinction between GyroAuth and existing location-based, continuous, adaptive, risk-based, and multi-factor authentication models.
topics: Comparison with WebAuthn, MFA, Zero Trust, behavioral biometrics, and risk-based authentication
Security properties that cannot be reduced to individual context signals
Relay, drift, sequence, scope, and transition attack models
Formal security claims and measurable evaluation criteria
future_research_themes.boundary: GyroAuth is not defined as GPS or Bluetooth authentication. Location and proximity may be input factors, but the core application evaluates convergence, deviation, stability, selection, and trajectory continuity.

future_research_themes.name: Theory Identity
future_research_themes.objective: Clarify the primary object and explanatory scope of Gyro Logic.
future_research_themes.research_question: What is the primary mathematical and theoretical object of Gyro Logic?
future_research_themes.boundary: Candidate viewpoints are not new Core elements. This theme must distinguish the invariant Core from interpretations, derived concepts, temporal descriptions, and implementation models.
